svg.text: build once · deploy many · private by default
A cascading platform: one source of truth → generated repos that stay current → repeatable, secret-less subscription onboarding.
THE CASCADE — a change flows downhill to everything that depends on it
a real app that CONSUMES the platform
the canary · the worked example · run what-if here before merge
a cookiecutter template that GENERATES repos
one command → a complete, wired-up service repo
GENERATE A REPO — cookiecutter creates, cruft keeps it current
cruft update → applies the diff as a patch
like rebasing onto the latest template
6 archetypes, each renders the right source + build template + Azure target:
dotnet-api · dotnet-web · python-function · go-web · go-desktop · node-agent
ONBOARD A SUBSCRIPTION — one script, three planes, zero stored secrets
Three planes need three tools — which is why onboarding is a script that calls Bicep, not Bicep alone. WIF = no SP password to rotate or leak.
SHIP CODE — build once, deploy many, ONE branch (main)
the SAME immutable artifact is promoted the whole way — no rebuild-per-env, no drift
The zip/image that passes dev is byte-for-byte
what reaches prod. Drift becomes impossible.
Infra promotes the same way — gated by `what-if`.
Each stage dependsOn the last + targets an ADO
Environment. Two audit surfaces: who merged (git)
PRIVATE BY DEFAULT → SELF-HOSTED AGENTS (the other half of the story)
Microsoft-hosted agents physically can't reach a private endpoint. The in-VNet self-hosted agent is the only thing that can deploy here.
